mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 310 in CPIAUCSL triggers central bank rate hikes, increasing discount rates and reducing equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 310 despite the signal.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in GDP (GDPC1) and industrial production (INDPRO) signals robust economic demand, driving corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show no significant correlation with combined GDPC1 and INDPRO trends (Rank IC < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates during a tightening cycle compress bond yields, diverting capital from equities and causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where FEDFUNDS rises by 0.5pp over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: Strong housing starts (HOUST) and real sales (RSAFS) indicate elevated consumer demand, boosting corporate revenues and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no significant correlation with combined HOUST and RSAFS trends (Rank IC < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) reflects improved supply chain efficiency and manufacturing activity, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no significant correlation with INDPRO trends (Rank IC < 0.03).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in payrolls (PAYEMS) and declining unemployment (UNRATE) signal a tightening labor market, increasing corporate pricing power and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no significant correlation with combined PAYEMS and UNRATE trends (Rank IC < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment (UNRATE) above 4% indicates deteriorating labor market conditions, reducing consumer spending and causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4% despite the signal.
